new_traits.name: Extrinsic Motivation
new_traits.description: Driven by external rewards such as compensation, status, and formal recognition.

new_traits.name: Intrinsic Motivation
new_traits.description: Driven by internal rewards, personal interest, and inherent task satisfaction.

new_traits.name: Autonomy Preference
new_traits.description: Desire to be self-directed and have strong control over one's own work and choices.

new_traits.name: Purpose Orientation
new_traits.description: Motivated by a desire to contribute to something larger than oneself and make a societal impact.

new_traits.name: Structural Preference
new_traits.description: Seeks defined, organized, and stable environments with clear hierarchical guidelines.

new_traits.name: Entrepreneurial Intent
new_traits.description: Inclination towards starting, building, and owning a discrete business or new venture.

new_traits.name: Career Readiness
new_traits.description: The degree to which an individual feels prepared, equipped, and strategically positioned to enter the professional world.

questions.question_text: Which type of work excites you the most?

mappings.reason: Demonstrates a strong internal drive toward logic, troubleshooting, and systematic resolution.
Suggests a preference for engaging with objects or systems rather than directly mediating human emotional needs.
Indicates core fulfillment is derived from positive interpersonal impact and active mentorship.
Implies an elevated capacity to understand and relate to the subjective states of others.
Signals a profound urge for originality, artistic expression, and generative thinking.
Suggests a low tolerance for highly procedural or rigidly standardized repetitive tasks.
Reveals a desire to take command, organize resources, and drive collective outcomes.
Indicates motivation tied to completing broader objectives and securing business execution.

frameworks.name: RIASEC
frameworks.relevance: Directly maps to the Investigative, Social, Artistic, and Enterprising interest orientations of the Holland Code framework.

questions.confidence_logic: Confidence in trait alignment is high here as fundamental work-task preferences typically predict deeper psychological predispositions with strong reliability.

contributions.personality: Reflects foundational personality structures aligned with how one engages with ideas, people, or systems.
contributions.swot: Establishes core domain operational strengths while pointing to vulnerabilities when assigned diametrically opposed functional roles.
contributions.4w1h: Defines the fundamental 'What' of the user's optimal operational environment.
contributions.strengths: Pinpoints the intrinsic capabilities that flow naturally without draining mental energy.
contributions.weaknesses: Highlights domains where motivation will quickly flag, requiring external scaffolding.
contributions.career: Creates a top-level filter for pursuing engineering, social services, design, or management pathways.
contributions.learning: Frames the optimal approach for skill acquisition, from isolated technical study to collaborative social learning.
contributions.roadmap: Acts as phase one for career-path alignment, dictating the first set of required vocational competencies.
contributions.opportunities: Directs the individual toward relevant internships, boot camps, or volunteering paradigms tailored to their core interest.
contributions.podcasts: Recommends functional-specific audio shows, like structural tech deep-dives or dynamic leadership interviews.
contributions.books: Informs reading list curation, ranging from software architecture to emotional intelligence theory.
contributions.role_models: Connects the individual with industry pioneers who excel in their exact domain of structural fascination.
contributions.motivation: Secures long-term engagement by assuring daily responsibilities map back to innate excitement profiles.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could include a fifth option blending these (e.g., product management) to capture integrative generalists.

questions.question_text: Which subject do you naturally enjoy learning?

mappings.reason: Demonstrates an affinity for empirical data, rigid logic framing, and systemic discovery.
Signals a desire to uncover the mechanisms of how the physical or digital world operates.
Indicates an innate interest in market mechanics, value exchange, and organizational economics.
Often correlates with a focus on metrics, scalability, and measurable growth.
Strongly correlated with intellectual openness to aesthetics, form, and unstructured abstraction.
Directly indicates a preference for generative visual, auditory, or sensory pursuits.
Suggests a cognitive interest in human behavior, cultural artifacts, and societal empathy.
Aligns with an aptitude for understanding complex sociological and psychological interactions.

frameworks.name: Big Five
frameworks.relevance: Connects directly to Openness to Experience, measuring appreciation for art, emotion, adventure, and unusual ideas versus data.

questions.confidence_logic: Natural intellectual enjoyment is a highly reliable proxy for cognitive traits; consistency across subjects builds valid Big Five models.

contributions.personality: Maps subject-matter affinities to foundational cognitive traits like Openness to Experience or orderliness.
contributions.swot: Strengths align with domain-specific deep knowledge, while threats arise from interdisciplinary gaps requiring cross-training.
contributions.4w1h: Clarifies the 'Why' behind their intellectual pursuit, revealing what holds their attention over long periods.
contributions.strengths: Identifies innate cognitive aptitudes, whether logic, aesthetics, empathy, or economic strategy.
contributions.weaknesses: Highlights potential disengagement vulnerabilities if forced to learn disconnected or uninteresting material.
contributions.career: Helps match candidates to specific knowledge-worker verticals like STEM, finance, or creative arts.
contributions.learning: Informs the choice of academic majors, certification routes, and self-directed syllabus design.
contributions.roadmap: Lays down the thematic groundwork for building a coherent portfolio of specialized knowledge.
contributions.opportunities: Suggests viable paths for further education, research assistantships, or thought-leadership writing.
contributions.podcasts: Recommends deep-dive educational podcasts tailored perfectly to their favorite intellectual domain.
contributions.books: Unlocks a curated library of non-fiction, ranging from economic history to cognitive psychology.
contributions.role_models: Ties the learner to academic or entrepreneurial titans who mastered the exact same intellectual space.
contributions.motivation: Leverages pure intellectual curiosity as an intrinsic driver, unblocking resistance to hard study.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could separate formal 'Science' and applied 'Technology' to differentiate theoretical researchers from applied engineers.

questions.question_text: Which task do you perform most confidently?

mappings.reason: Demonstrates high self-efficacy in untangling complex issues and deriving actionable solutions.
Requires the ability to systematically break down variables with confident precision.
Indicates high linguistic confidence and social comfort in front of audiences.
Suggests a preference for visible, highly interactive social dynamics.
Reflects a secure belief in one's own generative and artistic capabilities.
Often requires the proactive drive to start from a blank slate.
Displays confident authority in task delegation, team synchronization, and management.
Necessitates a strong grasp of collaborative dynamics and collective output metrics.

frameworks.name: VIA Strengths
frameworks.relevance: Maps directly to core character strengths, demonstrating whether the individual leans into cognitive (problem-solving) or interpersonal (leadership) dimensions.

questions.confidence_logic: Confidence in a specific task execution provides a reliable behavioral signal about past reinforcement and innate ability.

contributions.personality: Reveals whether the user favors rigorous solitary cognition versus bold interpersonal dynamics.
contributions.swot: Establishes their keystone strength, while pointing to delegable weaknesses outside their confidence zone.
contributions.4w1h: Defines the 'How'—the overarching behavioral mechanism by which they approach daily execution.
contributions.strengths: Anchors their core value proposition in a team, making task alignment incredibly clear.
contributions.weaknesses: Identifies tasks they may instinctively avoid due to deep-seated imposter syndrome or low self-efficacy.
contributions.career: Connects functional confidence to role titles, distinguishing analysts from PR reps or product managers.
contributions.learning: Suggests pivoting from basic tutorials to advanced, high-stakes practicums in their confident area.
contributions.roadmap: Advises building outward from this keystone strength to create a compelling, t-shaped professional profile.
contributions.opportunities: Signals immediate readiness to handle high-visibility tasks within their demonstrated comfort zone.
contributions.podcasts: Recommends tactical shows focused on refining their specific craft, like elite public speaking or systems design.
contributions.books: Highlights advanced technical or leadership literature to push them beyond merely 'confident' into 'masterful'.
contributions.role_models: Pairs them with executives who built empires leveraging the exact same functional superpower.
contributions.motivation: Drives self-assurance by reminding the user to consciously lean on the domains where they reliably succeed.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could frame options purely around verbs to strip away domain context and measure pure functional self-efficacy.

questions.question_text: Which work environment would make you happiest?

mappings.reason: Implies a search for routine, clear hierarchies, predictable schedules, and structured boundaries.
May indicate lower comfort with sudden, volatile changes in physical or operational settings.
Demonstrates a strong need for self-directed scheduling and independent workspace control.
Successfully navigating pure flexibility implies confidence in internal self-regulation.
Correlates heavily with a thirst for novel stimuli, diverse geographies, and dynamic day-to-day experiences.
Requires constant readjustment to new logistical situations and local cultures.
Signals a desire to operate in high-growth, high-ambiguity environments focused on disrupting norms.
Firms lacking deep institutional stability attract those comfortable with outcome variance.

frameworks.name: SDT
frameworks.relevance: Evaluates the degree to which autonomy over one's physical space and time drives overall well-being.

questions.confidence_logic: Environmental preferences are powerful identifiers of psychological safety needs, separating high-structure individuals from high-autonomy seekers.

contributions.personality: Reflects the introversion/extraversion and chaos-tolerance components of their workplace personality.
contributions.swot: Exposes environmental threats—such as putting a hyper-flexible worker in a rigid 9-to-5 corporate structure.
contributions.4w1h: Defines the 'Where' of their optimal productivity, clarifying locational and spatial needs.
contributions.strengths: Shows adaptability to specific constraints, such as thriving in chaotic travel or mastering remote self-discipline.
contributions.weaknesses: Highlights conditions that will reliably precipitate burnout, micro-management distress, or isolation.
contributions.career: Directly filters prospective employers by company culture, size, phase, and physical policy.
contributions.learning: Informs whether they should study via highly structured bootcamps or flexible, self-paced asynchronous courses.
contributions.roadmap: Helps negotiate working conditions early in the career planning process to ensure long-term retention.
contributions.opportunities: Points toward specific lifestyle-integrated roles like digital nomadism or traditional corporate ladders.
contributions.podcasts: Matches with shows exploring their lifestyle—such as remote-work optimization or startup building tactics.
contributions.books: Suggests operational literature, like 'Deep Work' for remote staff or 'The Lean Startup' for innovators.
contributions.role_models: Connects them to successful corporate CEOs, digital nomads, or field researchers depending on their choice.
contributions.motivation: Enhances daily drive by matching their biological and psychological rhythm to their physical surroundings.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could add a hybrid option to assess those who actively seek integration of structure and autonomy rather than one extreme.

questions.question_text: In a group project, you usually prefer to…

mappings.reason: Manifests a clear desire to take upfront responsibility for vision, motivation, and overall outcome.
Requires stepping up pro-actively to assume the burden of group direction.
Indicates a deep structural preference for managing logistics, timelines, and discrete task allocations.
Shows a propensity for building order out of group chaos.
Reveals a desire to uncouple personal performance from group dependencies.
Suggests slight resistance to deep synchronous collaboration in favor of siloed execution.
Evidences high adaptability and selflessness in filling operational gaps to ensure group success.
Shows willingness to subordinate personal ego to collective harmony.

frameworks.name: Big Five
frameworks.relevance: Differentiates high Agreeableness (support/coordination) from distinct assertiveness/extraversion (leadership).

questions.confidence_logic: Self-reported group dynamics powerfully reflect natural interpersonal hierarchies and comfort zones established through ongoing social feedback.

contributions.personality: Exposes traits related to social dominance, agreeableness, and introverted task-focus.
contributions.swot: Strengths lie in knowing their optimal team role; threats occur when forced into a diametrically opposed dynamic.
contributions.4w1h: Answers 'Who' they are within a collective, defining their functional persona amongst peers.
contributions.strengths: Highlights specific social utilities, such as visionary leadership, logistical genius, or flexible support.
contributions.weaknesses: Reveals blind spots, like an independent contributor struggling to communicate, or a leader bad at details.
contributions.career: Correlates exactly to job titles like Project Manager (B), Executive (A), IC Developer (C), or HR Support (D).
contributions.learning: Guides users toward learning leadership negotiation vs asynchronous deep-work tactics.
contributions.roadmap: Prepares them for behavioral interviews by explicitly branding their collaborative archetype.
contributions.opportunities: Identifies the perfect functional slot they should pitch themselves for in upcoming hackathons or cross-functional teams.
contributions.podcasts: Recommends group psychology or management podcasts to refine their chosen collaborative stance.
contributions.books: Points toward literature spanning from 'Radical Candor' for leaders to 'Quiet' for independent contributors.
contributions.role_models: Provides archetypal models like operational COOs vs visionary CEOs to emulate.
contributions.motivation: Increases job satisfaction by ensuring they pursue roles that grant them their preferred social dynamic.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could specify situational stakes, as some prefer leading low-stakes projects but contributing independently on high-stakes ones.

questions.question_text: If you had the opportunity, would you like to start your own business?

mappings.reason: Signals a profound, intrinsic, and unwavering desire for venture creation.
Requires high comfort with the acute financial and operational gambles inherent to startups.
Demonstrates awareness of current skill deficits but confidence in overcoming them through time.
Shows a measured but positive inclination toward eventual business ownership.

options.label: Only if the opportunity is right

mappings.reason: Implies a highly calculating, risk-adjusted approach to enterprise that requires evidentiary confidence.
Suggests apprehension toward leaping without a substantial safety net or guaranteed market.

options.label: No, I prefer working in an organization

mappings.reason: Indicates a deep psychological need for the safety, predictability, and infrastructure of existing entities.
Explicitly rejects the chaotic, autonomous burden of building from scratch.

frameworks.name: Growth Mindset
frameworks.relevance: Distinguishes between immediate unbridled risk, developmental patience, purely analytical risk-aversion, and fixed preference for safety.

questions.confidence_logic: Explicit intent regarding business creation is the strongest psychometric predictor of actual future entrepreneurial behavior and risk profiles.

contributions.personality: Exposes an individual's fundamental relationship with uncertainty, constraint, and systemic authority.
contributions.swot: Captures the strength of bold initiative versus the threat of uncalculated ruin, or the strength of stability vs the threat of stagnation.
contributions.4w1h: Frames the 'When' of their ultimate career ambition—now, later, conditionally, or never.
contributions.strengths: Identifies capacities for extreme self-reliance, calculated patience, or steady corporate loyalty.
contributions.weaknesses: Highlights potential impatience in rigid systems (for founders) or paralysis in chaotic systems (for employees).
contributions.career: Directly paths the user toward incubators, rotational management programs, or stable public sector roles.
contributions.learning: Informs whether they need rapid-fire founder bootcamps or long-term institutional MBAs.
contributions.roadmap: Alters the 5-year timeline from 'climbing the ladder' to 'building the ladder.'
contributions.opportunities: Signals readiness for co-founder networking, side-hustle creation, or safe corporate promotions.
contributions.podcasts: Splits recommendations between 'How I Built This' for dreamers and deep-industry strategy for company folks.
contributions.books: Suggests reading purely entrepreneurial texts versus nuanced intrapreneurial strategy guides.
contributions.role_models: Connects extreme risk-takers with startup legends, and cautious planners with enduring corporate CEOs.
contributions.motivation: Calibrates ultimate vision, preventing founders from languishing in cubicles, and averting the stress of pushing cautious people to jump.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could differentiate between 'freelancing/solo-preneurship' and 'building a scalable startup' to refine the exact type of business intent.

questions.question_text: Which industry interests you the most?

mappings.reason: Requires constant intellectual pivoting and a ravenous appetite for bleeding-edge adaptation.
Rooted heavily in algorithmic logic and systems optimization.
Driven by core biological or psychological scaffolding meant to heal or elevate other human beings.
Mandates high emotional intelligence to navigate complex human vulnerabilities.
Points directly to a fascination with economics, macro-trends, and wealth generation structures.
Often aligns with aggressive, metrics-driven professional cultures focused on yield.
Demonstrates an unwavering commitment to aesthetics, storytelling, and cultural production.
Thrives on fluid boundaries, cultural flux, and unconventional execution modes.

frameworks.name: RIASEC
frameworks.relevance: Translates overarching cognitive alignments (Realistic/Investigative vs Social vs Artistic) into concrete macro-economic sectors.

questions.confidence_logic: Broad industry selection reliably clusters multiple micro-behaviors into a coherent socio-economic identity.

contributions.personality: Maps complex trait combinations to overarching societal functions (e.g., tech innovator vs healer).
contributions.swot: Illuminates domain-specific superpower potential while highlighting vulnerabilities in opposed sectors.
contributions.4w1h: Defines the macro 'Where' of their entire professional narrative and ecosystem.
contributions.strengths: Correlates innate inclinations to the macroeconomic environments that reward those very traits.
contributions.weaknesses: Suggests friction points if they attempt to enter industries with conflicting core cultural rhythms.
contributions.career: Acts as the most direct vector for generating specific job boards, company targets, and networking events.
contributions.learning: Guides macro-academic choices, such as pursuing a BS vs a BA, or a Medical degree vs an MFA.
contributions.roadmap: Provides the foundational bedrock upon which a 10-year industry-specific ascent is mapped.
contributions.opportunities: Channels energy toward industry-specific conferences, publications, and niche guilds.
contributions.podcasts: Recommends exact macro-trend podcasts, like silicon valley news, healthcare policy, or design theory.
contributions.books: Unlocks vast, sector-specific reading lists from 'The Lean Startup' to 'The Aesthetics of Design'.
contributions.role_models: Aligns the user with iconic industry titans who define their chosen socio-economic sphere.
contributions.motivation: Triggers massive intrinsic engagement by assuring the user that their daily grind serves a broader sector they revere.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could allow for multi-select options as modern careers increasingly intersect (e.g., EdTech, HealthTech).

questions.question_text: How prepared do you feel for your future career?

mappings.reason: Indicates a high degree of subjective alignment, proactive planning, and acquired competence.
Demonstrates robust self-efficacy and belief in immediate professional execution capability.

options.label: I have some idea but need guidance

mappings.reason: Shows healthy introspection regarding skill gaps and an openness to external mentorship.
Reflects a transitional baseline state, requiring tactical support to cross the finish line.
Signals fluidity and a refusal to lock in prematurely before gathering sufficient life data.
Indicates active exploration over immediate rigid tactical execution.
Highlights a lack of formalized, targeted professional trajectory.

options.label: I don't know where to start

mappings.reason: Reveals complete paralysis or profound lack of exposure to career wayfinding mechanisms.
Suggests potential anxiety or deeply lowered self-efficacy regarding the transition to adulthood/work.

frameworks.name: Bloom's
frameworks.relevance: Differentiates users at the knowledge-gathering phase from those actively applying and evaluating their career strategies.

questions.confidence_logic: Self-assessment of readiness strongly correlates with actual proactive behaviors like resume building, networking, and interviewing.

contributions.personality: Reflects traits of conscientiousness vs neuroticism in the face of major life transitions.
contributions.swot: Exposes an immediate threat (paralysis) to mitigate, or an immediate strength (readiness) to exploit.
contributions.4w1h: Addresses the 'When' of their intervention needs, dictating urgency in career coaching.
contributions.strengths: Highlights self-assurance and proactive planning for those rating highly.
contributions.weaknesses: Pinpoints actionable blind spots, revealing exactly how much structural support the user requires.
contributions.career: Dictates whether they need foundational exploration exercises or highly targeted interview prep.
contributions.learning: Suggests moving from exploratory overviews to tactical hard-skills if already prepared.
contributions.roadmap: Completely alters the coaching timeline, providing a 101-level map versus a masterclass acceleration plan.
contributions.opportunities: Pairs prepared users with active hiring funnels, and unprepared users with foundational workshops.
contributions.podcasts: Recommends intense negotiation audio for the 'prepared' vs gentle career-discovery narratives for the 'lost'.
contributions.books: Ranges from tactical playbooks like 'Cracking the Coding Interview' to existential guides like 'Designing Your Life'.
contributions.role_models: Connects them with transparent mentors who openly discuss the messy reality of career transitions.
contributions.motivation: Either sparks urgency to break out of paralysis, or validates their hard work to maintain momentum.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could separate 'prepared with skills' from 'prepared with a network' to isolate the specific type of readiness.

questions.question_text: If you could choose your future career today, what would matter most?

mappings.reason: Demonstrates a heavy reliance on financial compensation and vertical prestige as primary drivers.
Correlates strongly with corporate ladder-climbing and competitive output metrics.
Signals that joy, emotional resonance, and alignment of tasks with inner values are paramount.
Highlights a refusal to compromise internal identity for external monetary reward.
Indicates a profound need to leave a benevolent, scalable legacy on society or local communities.
Requires elevating the welfare of others above pure personal enrichment.
Prioritizes sovereignty over time and energy, protecting personal well-being over corporate sacrifice.
Shows an understanding of personal limits and a desire to prevent systemic burnout.

frameworks.name: Ikigai
frameworks.relevance: Perfectly targets the intersecting nodes of what you love, what you are paid for, and what the world needs.

questions.confidence_logic: Value-based forced-choice questions are highly effective at stripping away socially desirable facades to reveal true bottom-line drivers.

contributions.personality: Refines the motivational profile, separating intensely driven individuals from harmony-seeking or philanthropic ones.
contributions.swot: Exposes core vulnerabilities—like an altruist burning out in finance, or a money-driven person languishing in a non-profit.
contributions.4w1h: Establishes the fundamental 'Why' guiding long-term life and career architecture.
contributions.strengths: Highlights resilient drivers; a purpose-driven person will withstand immense hardship if the cause is right.
contributions.weaknesses: Identifies conditions under which the individual will rapidly disengage or quit.
contributions.career: Dramatically alters target pools from Hedge Funds (A) to NGOs (C) to remote scale-ups (D).
contributions.learning: Frames the approach to education—whether viewing a degree as an ROI vehicle or an avenue for self-actualization.
contributions.roadmap: Helps design a 10-year trajectory that explicitly guards and maximizes their chosen core value.
contributions.opportunities: Points out tailored pathways, like impact investing, corporate sabbaticals, or high-growth equity tracks.
contributions.podcasts: Recommends financial independence pods, deep philosophical dialogues, or extreme leadership interviews.
contributions.books: Ranges from 'Your Money or Your Life' (D) to 'Man’s Search for Meaning' (C).
contributions.role_models: Aligns with specific archetypes: Elon Musk (A), Jane Goodall (C), or Tim Ferriss (D).
contributions.motivation: Acts as the ultimate safeguard; aligning tasks to this core value guarantees sustained, lifelong momentum.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could blend options in follow-up queries to see how much compensation an altruist is willing to sacrifice.

questions.question_text: What motivates you the most while choosing a career?

mappings.reason: Underscores a desire for the work itself to be inherently rewarding and intellectually stimulating.
Indicates a refusal to wear a corporate mask for the sake of survival.
Highlights pragmatic survival needs and a desire to neutralize economic anxiety.
Often correlates with individuals looking to safely provide for families or secure their base lifestyle.
Explicitly avoids volatile boom-or-bust career trajectories.
Driven by external validation, societal applause, and measurable hierarchical conquest.
Heavily reliant on ego-affirmation and status signals provided by corporate environments.
Anchors ambition in the betterment of the collective, emphasizing social impact metrics over personal wealth.
Demonstrates a moral imperative to ease suffering or build public goods.

frameworks.name: SDT
frameworks.relevance: Crisply differentiates between autonomous/intrinsic motivators (A, D) and controlled/extrinsic motivators (B, C).

questions.confidence_logic: Secondary validation of the previous question; consistent answers here solidify the user's core psychological anchor, locking in high-confidence motivational profiles.

contributions.personality: Resolves whether the individual operates from a locus of security, ego, altruism, or creative passion.
contributions.swot: Strengths lie in pure motivational durability aligned to the right job; threats arise if economic shocks hit a passion-seeker, or obscurity hits an ego-seeker.
contributions.4w1h: Further solidifies the 'Why', adding nuance between raw ambition and anxious stability.
contributions.strengths: Illuminates how to best incentivize the individual for peak performance.
contributions.weaknesses: Identifies exactly what levers will fail to inspire them (e.g., offering a title bump to someone who wants stability).
contributions.career: Steers final role selection toward secure civil work, high-risk sales, deep-tech research, or frontline non-profits.
contributions.learning: Informs how to market educational upskilling to the user—pitching ROI vs pitching community impact.
contributions.roadmap: Finalizes the criteria by which all future career pivots and offers must be rigorously evaluated.
contributions.opportunities: Points out niche opportunities that marry their chosen anchor with their demonstrated technical skills.
contributions.podcasts: Recommends exact podcast genres, ranging from stoic philosophy to aggressive wealth management.
contributions.books: Curates a concluding library that reinforces and protects their specific worldview from corporate cynicism.
contributions.role_models: Tethers them to specific public figures whose biographies prove that their chosen anchor is a viable path to a good life.
contributions.motivation: Operates as the master key to their psychological engine, guaranteeing that interventions are framed in their native motivational language.

questions.suggested_improvements: Could add a situational constraint (e.g., 'If your basic needs were already met...') to separate pure passion from forced stability.
